question: [File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getBalancesQuery()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the order parameter being directly interpolated into the SQL string (order by ab.account_id ${order}) by injecting SQL via the order query parameter, bypassing balanceFilterValidator() to extract gossip transaction data from the account_balance table?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getTokenAccountBalanceSubQuery()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the order parameter being directly interpolated into the token subquery (order by token_id ${order}) to inject SQL that extracts additional columns from token_account, revealing token transfer history linked to gossip transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getBalances()] Can an unprivileged user submit account.balance=<exact_amount> combined with timestamp=<T> to confirm whether a specific account held a precise balance at a given snapshot, allowing them to verify whether a gossip transaction of a known amount was received by that account?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getBalances()] Can an unprivileged user use account.balance=gte:<X>&account.balance=lte:<X> (exact balance match) combined with pagination to enumerate all accounts that received a specific gossip transaction amount, revealing the set of transaction recipients?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: formatBalancesResult()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the pagination link generation (utils.getPaginationLink()) with the anchorAccountId to enumerate all accounts in order, and by comparing balance snapshots across pages at two different timestamps, reconstruct the full set of accounts affected by a gossip transaction?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAccountBalanceTimestampRange()] Can an unprivileged user submit timestamp=ne:<ts1>&timestamp=ne:<ts2>&...&timestamp=ne:<tsN> with all known snapshot timestamps except one to force getAccountBalanceTimestampRange() to return exactly the one remaining snapshot, confirming a gossip transaction occurred at that specific timestamp?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getOptimizedTimestampRange()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the lte operator check (query.includes(utils.opsMap.lte)) which also matches the lt operator string, causing a timestamp=lt:<X> parameter to be misclassified as lte, resulting in an off-by-one error that includes an extra gossip transaction snapshot in the result?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getBalances()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that when pubKeyQuery is set but accountIdsQuery is null (no matching accounts), getBalances() returns early with an empty response, using this boolean oracle to determine whether a public key has any associated accounts that participated in gossip transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getOptimizedTimestampRange()] Can an unprivileged user submit timestamp=lte:<very_large_value> to set upperBound to constants.MAX_LONG, then observe the optimalLowerBound calculation to infer the current server time (nowInNs()), which can be used to time gossip transaction propagation attacks?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAccountBalanceTimestampRange()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that the treasury account (EntityId.systemEntity.treasuryAccount) is always present in every snapshot to use it as a reliable anchor for timestamp probing, enabling precise enumeration of all gossip transaction snapshot timestamps?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getTokenBalanceSubQuery()] Can an unprivileged user query with a narrow timestamp range to retrieve token_balance entries for a specific account, and by comparing token balances across two adjacent snapshots, infer the exact token amount transferred in a gossip transaction?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getOptimizedTimestampRange()] Can an unprivileged user submit timestamp=ne:<value> with a value that is a valid BigInt but outside the range of valid consensus timestamps, causing unexpected behavior in the neParams array that skips legitimate snapshot timestamps and hides gossip transaction evidence?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getBalancesQuery()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the tsParams array construction [upper, lower, upper, lower, upper] where upper and lower are used in both the token subquery and the main where clause, to craft a timestamp range that causes the token subquery to return data from a different time window than the main query, leaking cross-snapshot gossip transaction data?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getBalances()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the two different code paths in getBalances() (timestamp present vs. absent) to compare results and infer whether a gossip transaction occurred between the last snapshot and the current time, by observing differences in balance values?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getOptimizedTimestampRange()] Can an unprivileged user submit a timestamp=ne:<value> parameter where value is 0 or a very small number to probe the lower boundary of the balance snapshot table, revealing the earliest gossip transaction timestamp stored in the system?
